events.description: Join us every Wednesday evening for a space devoted to free-form movement, authentic expression, and the freedom to move through whatever is alive in you. This is your midweek pause — a moment to step out of the mind and drop fully into the wisdom of the body. Our dance floor is a sanctuary where emotion, breath, sound, sweat, and stillness all have a place. Here, you’re invited to explore the full spectrum of your human experience, expressed in real time through movement. Nothing to get right, nothing to perform. Just presence. Each week, our incredible DJs weave a magical, intentional sounds

events.name: Ice Cream Circus at Seneca
events.description: Lookingglass Theater's Ice Cream Circus! is an original, circus-infused play showcasing the transformative magic of a simple act of kindness. Conveyed without spoken language and entirely through dance, clown, and circus, Ice Cream Circus! is perfect for all generations and language backgrounds. The performance is free and open to the public. Bring your own chair! Presented by the Seneca Park and Lake Shore Park Advisory Councils.

events.name: Dancin' Fridays - Cumbia Lesson at Humboldt
events.description: Join instructor Itza Riedas for a vibrant cumbia dance lesson in the first hour, where you'll learn the lively rhythms and basic steps of this energetic Latin dance. Afterward, enjoy a fun-filled hour of social dancing in the park, celebrating community, music, and movement. All levels welcome!

events.name: Movies in the Parks at Lincoln
events.description: ANNIE 1982 Annie (1982) follows a spirited young orphan whose life changes when she’s invited to stay with billionaire Oliver Warbucks. Set during the Great Depression, the film blends humor, heart, and memorable musical numbers as Annie’s optimism touches those around her. PG | 128 minutes | 1982 *Closed Captioned Start time is approximate, all movies begin at dusk.

events.name: Water Safety Training at Oak St Beach
events.description: Join us for free safety orientations at your Chicago beach! Learn how to keep yourself and others safe at the beach, recognize a swimmer in distress, and how to respond in an emergency. Provides basic water safety training that will help all Chicagoans enjoy a safe summer at the beach or pool. Training is FREE! Registration optional.

events.name: Community Lakefront Powwow at Foster Beach
events.description: The American Indian Center of Chicago hosts Powwow at the Lake for its third year, bringing our community to the lakefront to gather. Dancers, drum groups, artists, and families come together for a full day of songs, dancing, food, and community. This is an intertribal powwow and everyone is welcome. Whether you’ve been around powwows or this is your first time, come spend the evening with us. aicchicago.org

events.name: Boxing Show at Humboldt
events.description: Open to all! Come cheer on our youth boxers as they compete against their peers from other parks. Are you a young boxer interested in participating? If you're between the ages of 8 and 25 and have participated in Chicago Park District boxing programs, talk to the staff at the park to register.

events.name: Yves Francois Rocambu Jazz at Smith
events.description: Since 2002 Yves Francois Rocambu Jazz has been entertaining audiences with its intoxicating mix of jazz horns, Afro-Caribbean rhythms, and Mediterranean melodies and dance. Their album Viva Rocambu! was released in 2023, and a new album is in the works for summer 2026 release. https://yvesfrancoisritmo.com/rocambu-jazz

events.name: Skate into Summer at Lake Shore
events.description: Kick off this summer at Lake Shore Park with a FREE indoor roller skating party! We will have a limited supply of skates available to borrow.

events.name: Soccer Island at Berger
events.description: Soccer Island is a transfuturist play written and directed by Gabriel Chalfin-Piney-González. The play details a small group of people stranded on a floating soccer field, balancing over the ocean. Through a series of choreographed movements, oral history bound monologues the group grapples with sustenance and sanity in a timeless world. This play is written for an adult audience but is suitable for all ages. events.name: RiverLab: Family Paddle at River
events.description: Join us for a relaxed kid-friendly paddle on the Chicago River, perfect for paddlers of any skill level. This guided canoe paddle offers a chance to explore the Chicago River while enjoying a fun outing on the water. Be sure to wear comfortable clothing and remember to bring water and sunscreen. All equipment is included in your registration fee. Basic instruction will be provided; beginners are encouraged to join! Please plan to arrive 15 minutes before the program start time. Youth under 18 must be accompanied by an adult. The Chicago Park District's RiverLab program is a series of education

events.name: Empowerment and Connection Through Piñatas at Margate
events.description: The activity is to make our own piñatas, to take the time to make something for ourselves, whether it will last or not. First participants will be guided to write down their own accomplishments or affirmations to themselves, big or small, onto strips of paper. Once done, participants will keep that to the side and start making their own piñatas.

events.name: RiverLab: Summer Float Paddle at River
events.description: Summer Float offers a chance to relax and take in the beauty of the Chicago River while keeping a slow and enjoyable pace. Expect frequent breaks, be sure to wear comfortable clothing, and remember to bring water and sunscreen. All equipment is included in your registration fee. Basic instruction will be provided; beginners are encouraged to join! Please plan to arrive 15 minutes before the program start time. The Chicago Park District's RiverLab program is a series of educational events and activities dedicated to reconnecting residents of Chicago with their river.

events.description: Baba Yaga’s Stew brings fun, free contra dances to Chicago parks—with irresistible grooves, soaring melodies and bubbling laughter. No experience or partner necessary–there will be a caller who will teach each dance so anyone can jump in! Experienced dancers will help newcomers, and neighbors will get a chance to experience their park in a new way. The band plays songs from all over the world, Ireland, Thailand, Romania, Peru, India and more, celebrating the diverse ethnic heritage of the United States. events.name: Shakespeare Blood Oath: Henry VIII at River
events.description: Shakespeare Blood Oath has vowed to perform every one of Shakespeare’s plays, in an order we determined by pulling them all out of a hat. Well, it’s finally time to do a bad one, one even Shakespeare wasn’t proud of — Henry VIII. Frankly, we couldn’t be more excited! This year, SBO is injecting the chaos and energy of short-form improv into Shakespeare, with games, audience participation, and more. The biggest twist … the SBO players will rehearse to perform every role, and each performance will be cast in front of the audience at the beginning of every show! https://www.shakespearebloodoath.c

Join us for live animal interactions, nature scavenger hunts, and music performances from Old Town School of Folk Music.

events.description: THE PRINCESS DIAIRIES A shy American teenager learns she is heir to the throne of a European kingdom. Share fascinating views of the surface of the Moon, Sun, Planets and explore the mysteries of the cosmos with Astronomers and fans. Family Friendly and a free activity for all sky lovers.

events.description: Explore the wonders of the night sky with Chicago Park’s premier astronomy event series. Join the Chicago Astronomer for guided stargazing sessions using professional-grade telescopes, perfect for beginners and enthusiasts alike. events.description: WATER FOR ELEPHANTS A young veterinary student during the Great Depression who joins a struggling circus, caring for its animals and forming a deep bond with a performer, leading to a dangerous love triangle.
